subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Fever: The Body's Thermostat
description: A fast breakdown of Fever: The Body's Thermostat for builders.

hook.text: A fever isn't the disease. It's your body deliberately raising the temperature to fight back.

buildup.text: The hypothalamus raises your body's set point in response to infection signals.

discovery.text: Higher temperatures boost immune cell activity and make it harder for pathogens to multiply.

twist.text: Moderate fevers under 103°F may actually speed recovery—cooling them isn't always helpful.

climax.text: High fevers above 104°F become dangerous and need medical intervention.

punchline.text: Low fever: your body fighting. High fever: your body losing.

quiz.question: Why does the body produce a fever during infection?
options: To boost immune activity and slow pathogen growth
Because the immune system has failed
To signal pain to the brain
